mainEntity.name: How much does healthcare cleaning cost in Massachusetts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Healthcare facility cleaning in Massachusetts typically ranges from $75 to $300+ per visit, depending on facility size, service frequency, and scope. Daily medical office cleaning may average $150-$250 per visit. Specialty terminal cleaning starts at $300+. Call (978) 307-8107 for a free customized quote.

mainEntity.name: What factors affect healthcare cleaning pricing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pricing depends on: facility size in square feet, daily patient volume, frequency (daily/weekly/monthly), facility type (medical office, surgery center, nursing home), required protocols (terminal cleaning, ATP testing), and specialty disinfectants (List N, List H, List K). On-call biohazard response is priced separately.

mainEntity.name: Do you offer monthly contracts or pay-per-visit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both. Monthly contracts include scheduled visits at a flat rate with quality control walkthroughs. Pay-per-visit is available for occasional terminal cleaning, biohazard response, or special projects. Most facilities save 15-25% with monthly contracts vs ad-hoc service.

mainEntity.name: Is the facility assessment really free?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Our 20-minute facility walkthrough and written quote are 100% free with zero obligation. We also offer a free ATP bioluminescence surface test for the first 3 Massachusetts healthcare facilities each month.

mainEntity.name: What is included in the price?

acceptedAnswer.text: Every quote includes: trained clinical cleaning technicians (bloodborne pathogens certified), EPA-registered hospital-grade disinfectants, color-coded microfiber cleaning supplies, all equipment, $2,000,000 liability insurance coverage, MA HIC #213341 licensed contractor, written cleaning verification logs, and quality control supervision.

mainEntity.name: Do you offer discounts for multiple facilities or referrals?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Multi-facility healthcare networks receive volume pricing. Existing clients who refer a new healthcare facility receive a service credit. Annual contracts save 5-10% vs month-to-month. Call (978) 307-8107 to discuss.

What Affects Your Healthcare Cleaning Price?

Custom pricing is calculated based on six key factors. Understanding these helps you compare quotes accurately:

Facility Size

Square footage drives base labor hours. Larger facilities have lower per-sq-ft cost due to economies of scale.

Service Frequency

Daily cleaning costs more in absolute dollars but less per visit. Weekly contracts have higher per-visit pricing.

Facility Type

Surgery centers and dialysis clinics need terminal cleaning. Medical offices need standard infection control.

Specialty Protocols

ATP testing, EPA List H disinfectants for C. auris, terminal cleaning add to base price.

Documentation Level

Joint Commission and AAAHC accreditation prep includes detailed audit trails — adds 5-10% to base.

Emergency Response

24/7 biohazard cleanup, COVID outbreak response, and post-flood disinfection priced per incident.

What's Always Included In Every Quote

Unlike commercial cleaning quotes that hide costs in fine print, every Dory's Cleaning quote includes these protections at no additional charge:

  • $2,000,000 Liability Insurance: Coverage included on every job. We provide certificates of insurance to clients on request.
  • MA HIC #213341 License: Massachusetts-licensed contractor. No subcontracting to unlicensed workers.
  • Bloodborne Pathogens Certified Staff: All technicians complete OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1030 training annually before clinical deployment.
  • EPA-Registered Hospital-Grade Disinfectants: List N, List H, List K products. Documented kill claims and contact times.
  • All Equipment & Supplies: Color-coded microfiber, HEPA vacuums, EPA disinfectants, PPE. We bring everything.
  • Written Cleaning Verification Logs: Date, time, areas serviced, products used. For DPH inspections and accreditation audits.
  • Quality Control Supervision: Monthly walkthroughs by our QC supervisor included in monthly contracts.

Healthcare Cleaning vs Standard Janitorial — Why The Cost Difference?

Healthcare facility cleaning costs more per square foot than standard office cleaning because of clinical training, specialized products, and documentation requirements. Here's the breakdown:

Feature Standard Janitorial Healthcare Cleaning
Cost per visit $30-$80 $75-$300+
Bloodborne pathogen training OSHA Certified
EPA Hospital-grade disinfectants List N/H/K
Color-coded microfiber Cross-contamination prevention
Compliance documentation DPH/Joint Commission ready
ATP verification testing Hospital-grade tech
Insurance coverage $500K typical $2,000,000

The "extra" cost of healthcare cleaning is what protects you from healthcare-associated infections (HAIs), regulatory fines, and accreditation failures.
